title: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD
display_title: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD
summary:
[]: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD?             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Wed Aug 06, 2008 7:47 pm I mean brick-and-mortar stores. Wal-Mart had it for $35 but now they've mysteriously raised the price to $80.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Aug 06, 2008 7:59 pm I got my box set off some random dude at the mall for $40, and he threw in Appleseed for free.

[]:  &n

chunks.content:
Article: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD
[]: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD?             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Wed Aug 06, 2008 7:47 pm I mean brick-and-mortar stores. Wal-Mart had it for $35 but now they've mysteriously raised the price to $80.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Aug 06, 2008 7:59 pm I got my box set off some random dude at the mall for $40, and he threw in Appleseed for free.

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Wed Aug 06, 2008 10:54 pm Well, internet searches on store websites would help. A lot of times, they advertise prices of stuff in stock at the store with equal prices between the actual store and the warehouse. I suggest looking around. I paid about $60 for my set. Could definitely go lower than what I paid if I put more effort into it. How much lower, I'm not sure.

[]:             Quote Post by Captain Mister » Thu Aug 07, 2008 12:19 am I bought my set several years ago for $40 on eBay. However, it's 4 disc: 3 for the series and 1 for all the movies. http://www.discountanimedvd.com/detail.asp?dvdno=18201&title=Evangelion,%20Neon%20Genesis:%20TV%20+%20Movies%20(DVD%20Box%20Set,%20English%20Dubbed,%20FX)

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Thu Aug 07, 2008 12:27 am POSTING NINJA STRIKES AGAIN! Captain Mister, that link is very large. Most people think of such links as an eyesore. To cut down on the length, try posting it as text. This is the way you do it: [url=http://your_url_here:dxtv5zlx] your text here[/url (Don't forget to add the last bracket; I omitted it so you can see the bbc code) Try it!

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Thu Aug 07, 2008 3:58 pm Probably went up in price because ADV's about to go belly up. You can still buy it for around $40 through vendors on Amazon. The expensive one is the thinpack in a tin. Just get the regular thinpack.

Article: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Aug 07, 2008 7:52 pm Captain Mister wrote: Leave it to eBay to sell something bootlegged and advertise it as legit. At least its region free. I'll buy a more genuine copy of the director's cuts later on.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Thu Aug 07, 2008 11:47 pm [url]http://www.advfilms.com/[/url] Wait a couple months...

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sat Aug 09, 2008 6:09 am The cheapest place? Mandarake, or Animate in Japan. Depending on how old the DVD you are buying, it can range from half the original price for the newest, to something like a hundred yen if it was one of the first copies available (ofcourse, which the version is still in production- if not, the price goes up, not waht we want here)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:35 pm If you have any local Best Buys I'd check them. I just saw two sets at my local place for $55. I remember it was $27 on Amazon for a short while, but it seems to be out of stock now. The real trick is finding the film.

[]:             Quote Post by Raptus » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:45 pm Ebaaaay Fo sho

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Aug 12, 2008 6:47 pm Yeah, online. Way cheaper than in a store, unless you stumble upon an awesome pawnshop or something...

[]:             Quote Post by Iron Rooster » Tue Aug 12, 2008 10:02 pm ^ Would this pawnshop happen to have human organs? I could use another lung or two. Maybe a second heart might come in hand too. Are you still set on a brick and mortar store? I think it's really hard to find deals on NGE outside the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 12, 2008 10:03 pm The problem about asking online about brick and mortar stores

Article: Cheapest place to buy Eva on DVD
 really hard to find deals on NGE outside the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Tue Aug 12, 2008 10:03 pm The problem about asking online about brick and mortar stores is that... it really depends on what's in your area. I'd look up every major retailer and specialist you could. Simply call around and see what you can find.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Aug 12, 2008 11:23 pm Iron Rooster wrote: ^ Would this pawnshop happen to have human organs? I could use another lung or two. Maybe a second heart might come in hand too. Are you still set on a brick and mortar store? I think it's really hard to find deals on NGE outside the internet.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Aug 13, 2008 8:32 am Go to Japan. So many places that are cheaper than the internet. The internet, especially if sold by individeuals, are trying to get as much profit, but in places like Mandarake, they try to buy it as cheap as possible, and the price they sell at is not that different (around 10 yen or something like that) and so will be cheaper. I once saw around 3 2007 NGE complete box set sold for two thousand yen each (approx. £10, or $20), and NGE mangas costing 200 yens each (approx. £1 or $2)
